title: Thai Herbal Fondue Pot Dessert
description: A pillowy and show-off Thai dessert featuring kanom-ko, a traditional Thai coconut jelly dessert, with a twist of herbal flavors. This recipe is perfect for a dinner party or special occasion.
instructions: Combine coconut milk, sugar, and water in a saucepan and heat until the sugar dissolves.
Remove from heat and add dissolved agar agar and stir to combine.
Add chopped Thai basil leaves and Thai herbal tea to the mixture and stir to combine.
Strain the mixture through a fine-mesh sieve into a clean bowl.
In a separate bowl, whip coconut gel until it becomes light and fluffy.
Add lime juice, salt, gelatin, and pandan extract to the coconut gel and whip until well combined.
Pour the coconut gel mixture into the agar agar mixture and stir to combine.
Pour the mixture into a fondue pot or a small saucepan with a heat-proof glass bowl.
Place the fondue pot over a sous-vide water bath set to 160°F (71°C) and let it cook for 1 hour.
Remove the fondue pot from the sous-vide water bath and let it cool to room temperature.
Refrigerate the fondue pot for at least 2 hours or overnight before serving.
To serve, dip fresh fruits, cookies, or marshmallows into the coconut jelly dessert.
